summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all requirements: glob-based recursive file discovery, regex searching, preview mode (verified to not mutate files), backup creation and content correctness, replace mode with summary fields, glob isolation (*.txt vs *.log), and the summary report formatting. Fixture management is exemplary — BeforeAll/AfterAll for read-only Describe blocks, BeforeEach/AfterEach for mutating ones, and typed helper functions that follow the same strict-mode conventions as the implementation. Tests verify behaviors from multiple angles (return values, file content on disk, backup file existence and content) and include error-path checks (non-existent paths throw). Rigor loses one point for missing: replacement strings containing regex metacharacters, capture-group back-references ($1), empty files, files with BOM or non-UTF8 encoding, read-only files, and no test that a multi-match-per-line scenario is handled correctly (all current fixtures have at most one match per line). The summary tests rely entirely on in-memory PSCustomObject arrays rather than exercising the full pipeline, which is fine for unit isolation but leaves the integration between replace and summary untested as a sequence. Overall a trustworthy suite that would catch most realistic regressions.
